---
title: "Signpost Monitoring — Marseille TFPB"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(here)
```

## Widening Method: Signpost Monitoring

We identify five key external uncertainty factors that could materially affect 
TFPB revenues over the next three years, with observable signposts and alert thresholds.

```{r signpost-table}
signposts <- data.frame(
  Factor = c(
    "Cadastral revaluation reform",
    "Real estate market",
    "State grants (DGF)",
    "Structural vacancy",
    "TLV extension"
  ),
  Signpost = c(
    "Parliamentary calendar; DGFiP simulation reports",
    "DVF transaction volumes; Notaires de France price index",
    "PLF budget law DGF allocation; OFGL fiscal reports",
    "LOVAC annual update: structural vacancy stock",
    "JORF decrees; DGCL circulars"
  ),
  Alert_threshold = c(
    "Reform enacted with < 3 year horizon",
    "Price decline >5% OR volume drop >15%",
    "DGF cut >3% in real terms",
    "Structural rate > 4.5% OR stock >16,000 units",
    "TLV extended to > 50% of Marseille arrondissements"
  ),
  Strategic_implication = c(
    "Accelerate base modernisation strategy before reform locks in new values",
    "Reduce reliance on base growth; prioritise rate or vacancy levers",
    "Increase urgency of vacancy mobilisation to offset grant losses",
    "Intensify ANAH partnerships and vacancy taxation enforcement",
    "Rebalance toward rate increase if vacancy taxation revenue declines"
  )
)

kable(signposts, 
      col.names = c("Uncertainty factor", "Signpost", 
                    "Alert threshold", "Strategic implication"),
      caption = "Signpost monitoring table — Marseille TFPB strategy",
      booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "scale_down", "hold_position")) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "2.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "3.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(3, width = "3cm") %>%
  column_spec(4, width = "4cm")
```
